title: Neuropathy follow-up

examination.text: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped nerve-pain, pain or burning, functional, sleep, mood, response, clinician-decided plan, review, and referral fields without medicine names or doses.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.

source.title: Neuropathic pain in adults: pharmacological management in non-specialist settings

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — assessment, treatment planning, referral, and review

investigations.text: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped adult sensory distribution, progression, metabolic context, sensation, reflex, power, gait, B12, thyroid, renal, and referral fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

source_limitations: The workflow identity covers generic neuropathy, while the primary source is neuropathic pain and both sources are adult-only.

Vibration or monofilament testing, foot inspection or care, HbA1c wording, nerve-conduction studies, blood pressure, generic blood tests, dose titration, fixed follow-up, and the structured history draft remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, treatment choice, medicine dosing, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
